title: 3 takeaways from the Revolution’s 2-1 win over Minnesota United
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The New England Revolution secured a 2-1 victory over Minnesota United at Gillette Stadium, with goals from Carles Gil and Luca Langoni.
Matt Turner delivered crucial saves to secure the win, responding to criticism after a midweek error against Nashville.
Luca Langoni's decisive goal was the result of effective counterattack pressure, illustrating the Revolution's tactical strengths under coach Marko Mitrović.

title: SNL recap: Best moments from Will Ferrell hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’ season finale
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Will Ferrell hosted 'SNL' season 51 finale with Paul McCartney as musical guest on May 16, 2026.
Ferrell opened the show as Jeffrey Epstein's ghost, sharing scenes with James Austin Johnson's Donald Trump.
Paul McCartney performed three songs, including 'Days We Left Behind' and 'Band on the Run.'

title: Flashback: A look at previous times Tall Ships have sailed into Boston
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Sail Boston 2026 will occur from July 11-16, featuring tall ships from over 25 countries as part of Sail250.
Sail Boston events have been held in the years 1976, 1980, 1992, 2000, 2009, 2012, and 2017.
Queen Elizabeth II visited Boston in 1976 during a tall ships event, marking the first visit by a reigning British monarch.

title: Vibe in Patriots building reportedly better than two weeks ago with Mike Vrabel
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Mike Vrabel returns to Patriots rookie minicamp, reportedly improving team morale post-scandal.
Patriots making significant offseason moves, potentially trading for wide receiver A.J. Brown.
Kevin Byard praises Vrabel's coaching energy, likening him to his time in Tennessee.

title: Willson Contreras’s go-ahead HR, Payton Tolle’s 8 strong innings lift Red Sox past Braves 3-2
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Willson Contreras hit a decisive two-run homer in the eighth inning, leading the Red Sox to a 3-2 win over the Braves.
Red Sox's Payton Tolle pitched a career-high eight innings, allowing two runs and four hits, with three strikeouts.
Aroldis Chapman secured the save despite a bases-loaded situation in the ninth, contributing to Boston's comeback victory.

title: Red Sox place Trevor Story on injured list with sports hernia, contemplating surgery
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Trevor Story placed on the 10-day injured list with a sports hernia by Red Sox.
Nick Sogard recalled from Triple-A Worcester as Story's replacement on the roster.
Story's recovery plan undecided; possible surgery could sideline him for 6-10 weeks.

title: One driver dead in NH collision between pickup truck and school bus
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A 74-year-old man died after his pickup truck collided head-on with a school bus in Marlborough, NH.
The crash involved a bus carrying 13 students from Monadnock Regional Middle High School; no serious injuries reported.
The crash is under investigation by New Hampshire State Police and local authorities; witnesses are asked to contact them.

title: Carl Yastrzemski’s grandson, Braves’ Mike Yastrzemski, walks off Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Mike Yastrzemski gave the Braves a 3-2 win over the Red Sox with a 10th-inning walk-off hit.
Red Sox pitcher Connelly Early allowed two home runs over five innings, striking out six and walking none.
Boston fell to a season record of 18-26, struggling with runners in scoring positions, leaving seven on base.

title: Report: Reunion between Patriots, Stefon Diggs viewed as unlikely despite mutual interest
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Patriots' potential reunion with Stefon Diggs unlikely due to pending trade for A.J. Brown.
Stefon Diggs interested in rejoining Patriots, but salary cap constraints complicate his return.
Patriots face challenges integrating A.J. Brown and keeping Diggs due to cap space and team roles.

title: Boston police seek missing boy, 15
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Lafayette LaGrant-Gomes, 15, was last seen near 965 Blue Hill Ave in Dorchester on Tuesday around 1 p.m.
LaGrant-Gomes is described as a Black male, 5'8", with a thin build, brown eyes, and black hair.
Police request public assistance and ask anyone with information to contact District B-3 detectives or submit anonymous tips.
